meaning: n.手钻adj.有钻孔能力的v.用手钻钻
examples: His glance was like a gimlet, cold and piercing.
He would have said the same, with his unwavering gimlet glare, 50 years later.
She was kind to Connie, and tried to worm into her woman's soul with the sharp gimlet of her well-Born observations.
Up to half of these gimlet-eyed workers will have given their services free.
Collectors did not need to be so gimlet-eyed in the 19th century.
